九月文化

The Ninth Moon

Not tours. Fellowships.

Cultural immersions in China & Hong Kong

I'm Emily Lau — Grammy-winning musician, quadrolingual weirdo, and someone loving, detailed, culturally specific, and deeply worldly. I have been doing this for a decade. I have friends everywhere. You will feel it when you travel with me.

What I believe is simple: we don't need more money or more reasons to be angry or anxious. We need more understanding, more loosening, more indulgence that doesn't come at anyone's expense, more gratitude. China and America are not so different after all — but there is so much to exchange, and so much to enrich each other with. That's what world peace looks like to me. And that's what this trip is for.

The throughline

Every journey is built on the same foundation: these are my friends. The tea farmers, the calligrapher, the chefs, the hotel owners. You are not only being taken to attractions. You are being introduced to people — real people, in their real lives — who will become part of yours. Your only job is to arrive permeable. To let joy and wonder and strangeness come in and wake something up again. Leave the rest to me.

How we travel

This fellowship uses a maximalist, joyful, inclusive, and pragmatic lens to see the world — rooted in the belief that we are all connected. We always prioritize putting money and resources in the hands of the local people who guide and serve us.

Throughout the journey: ample original talks and hands-on demonstrations about the history, culture, food, people, and stories of exactly where we are. By the time we walk out the door each day, you're not a tourist. You're someone who understands what they're looking at.

Good to know

Before every journey, I do a comprehensive check-in with each traveler — your concerns, your body, your curiosity, your limits. I group people deliberately. I think carefully about who will bring out the best in whom. Most people return from these trips as dear friends. That part is not an accident.

On language
You don't need Chinese. Cities are enormous, friendly, and well-connected. Get WeChat, Alipay, an eSIM, and a translation app. Learn a few phrases if you'd like — or more, if you're inspired. And also: I take care of you.
On being noticed
In Hong Kong you blend in easily. In mainland China you may be stared at — and so will the pretty person next to you, and the old man across the street, and the baby in the carrier. It's a live-and-let-live culture in the most intoxicating sense. You can sing loudly, dance wildly, wear an insane costume in public at any hour — and no one will judge you. China is full of life and possibility in a way that is genuinely rare in the world.
On air quality
This is not the China of twenty years ago. Most cars are electric. Air quality in Shunde, Lijiang, and Hong Kong is generally excellent. Second-hand smoke exists in places — but clean air does not require a mask.
On safety
These are mega-cities with 20 million people, public transport everywhere, and police who will genuinely help you if needed. You cannot get lost. This is my home turf — I know the rules, the back doors, and who to call.
On money and prices
Most people are just living their lives — China is not out to get you. For big purchases, take my advice. I know real prices and real people.
On politics and speech
Tourists talk openly and freely. What you may find is that certain questions Americans carry — about Tibet, about Xinjiang — land with confusion rather than alarm. Not because people can't answer, but because the framing feels foreign to daily life. Regular people are living their lives. They are not monitoring yours. This is equally true in Hong Kong, where people talk loudly and freely about everything.
On feeling at home
You are not on a bus with 40 strangers. You are my guest. My aunt, my brother, my mom, and my high school friends will be amongst the smiley faces that greet you.
On visas
For most American passport holders: you can enter mainland China under the 10-day visa-free transit scheme — which covers this entire tour. Canadian, Mexican, and European passport holders are visa-free for 30 days in mainland China. All of these passports also allow entry to Hong Kong without a visa. If you have questions about your specific situation, just ask — I'm happy to advise.

You're not buying a tour.
You're buying time inside a world most people never find — because they didn't know who to ask.

Signature journey · 2026

Life-Nourishing Tour of Mainland China

November 19–29, 2026 · Single occupancy throughout · 3–4 or 7–9 persons
Latest departure from the USA: November 17

Day 1
Arrival — Hong Kong to Shunde
Meet at 9:15am at TASTE restaurant, IBIS Sheung Wan/Central, Hong Kong. Ferry to Shenzhen, lunch at terminal. Luxury van transfer to Shunde, Foshan — UNESCO City of Gastronomy. Afternoon massage treatments. Welcome dinner.
Day 2
Gardens & Body
Morning dim sum banquet with tea. Visit He Yuan, a masterpiece of classical Lingnan garden design. Afternoon rotation of treatments: hair therapy, ear therapy, massage, facial, teeth cleaning. Dinner. Evening dancing and singing in the square — highly encouraged.
Day 3
The Body Check
In-depth whole-body check-up at a five-star, Mayo Clinic alliance hospital. Includes chest CT, blood panel, liver and kidney function, contrast ultrasound of multiple organs, OB-GYN, EKG, eye test, and more — details upon consultation. A $12,000 value in the US, if you can even get an appointment. Takes 2-3 hours. Afternoon biking through Shunfeng Park, one of the largest parks in Asia. Extraordinary dinner.
Day 4
Art & Follow-Up
Breakfast at a beloved local garden bakery. Visit He Contemporary Art Gallery. Doctor follow-up consultation available. More treatments. Meals at local favorites.
Day 5
Into the Mountains
Free morning in Shunde. Fly to Lijiang, Yunnan — an ancient Naxi city cradled at the base of the Himalayas. Transfer to our hotel: a wooden fairyland perched above the ancient city. Welcome dinner with local hosts.
Day 6
Market Day
Morning in Lijiang's ancient market — herbs, teas, textiles, local produce, things you cannot find anywhere else. Afternoon at leisure. Gentle walking, tea, rest.
Day 7
Tiger Leaping Gorge
Full day at Tiger Leaping Gorge — one of the deepest gorges on earth, the Jinsha River thundering far below. Morning hike along the trail. Lunch at an internet-famous café overlooking the gorge. Return to Lijiang for dinner and rest.
Day 8
Remote Temple & Giant Lake
Drive to a remote Tibetan temple — quiet, ancient, and far from the tourist trail. Then onward to a giant mountain lake: horseback riding along the shore for those who want it, coffee or tea by the water for those who don't. Dinner at a local Tibetan restaurant — yak hotpot, one of the most singular meals of your life.
Day 9
Shangri-La altitude permitting
For those without altitude sensitivity — day trip to Shangri-La (Zhongdian), the Tibetan town at 3,200m. Ancient monastery, prayer wheels, butter tea, the roof of the world. Return to Lijiang by evening. This day is always optional — we assess as a group.
Day 10
Return
Transfer to Shenzhen. The tour concludes at Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport — fly home from there, or stay on and keep exploring (I can advise on extending your trip).
Land only, with in-depth body check $4,200
Land only, without body check $3,800

Price includes all lodging (single occupancy), nearly all meals, all treatments, activities, in-country flights, and ground transport. International flights to Hong Kong and home from Shenzhen are not included.
No visa required if not traveling further in China after the tour.

Optional services
✦ Flight booking & night-before hotel — arranged at cost
✦ Advice on extending your stay or visa procurement

How it works

Each journey begins with a theme, a season, and a small group of curious humans — carefully matched. The itinerary is a framework, not a script. Every day is shaped by weather, energy, and what's alive in the room.

The product is not the hotel room (although we always stay in rather beautiful ones). It is access to a world most people never find — because they didn't know who to ask.

Get in touch

Inquiries, questions, or just curious — write to singingemily@gmail.com

Not a travel agency. A portal into a new world — if you let it.
